design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate through horror-themed levels shooting enemies while managing ammunition and power-ups, progressing through five worlds to save the town
design_philosophy.primary_tension: 360-degree combat awareness in varied enemy configurations across themed environments
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Action-oriented horror movie protagonist defending against supernatural threats with diverse weapons
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game was developed as an unrelated title retrofitted with Zombies Ate My Neighbors license mid-development to boost sales; outsourced to Motion Pixel (Malaysian studio) with minimal original ZAMN team involvement, creating a mechanically similar but tonally distinct sequel

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up collection system (maintains ZAMN engine mechanics)
replayability_hooks: Horror movie homage content encourages replays for thematic discovery

world_building_techniques: Horror exhibit premise connecting five themed worlds
Cartoony art style creating tonal distance from traditional horror

faction_indicators: Zeke and Julie (protagonists from ZAMN) as established characters
danger_signals: Horror exhibit curse threatening the town
Supernatural monster encounters across themed worlds
environmental_storytelling: Five distinct horror-themed worlds creating visual progression
Cartoony aesthetic subverting horror tropes for approachable action

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (development context, licensing details)
wikipedia - Ghoul Patrol article (gameplay description, critical reception, development outsourcing)
